name: Morningstar, Inc.
business: Morningstar, Inc. is a leading global provider of independent investment insights. It offers a variety of products and solutions that serve a wide range of market participants, including data, research, ratings, and software platforms through segments such as Morningstar Direct Platform, PitchBook, Morningstar Credit, Morningstar Wealth, and Morningstar Retirement.
sellsTo: Retirement plan providers, advisors, and sponsors

exposures.sentence: We launched the Morningstar MCP server, providing turnkey AI-connectivity of Morningstar data, research, and functionality including investment screening and fund holding lookups.
Alliances and redistributors (including global fintech firms)

Our business requires that we securely collect, process, store, and transmit confidential information including sensitive personal information relating to our operations, customers, employees and other third parties.

catalysts.sentence: On February 2, 2026, we completed our previously announced acquisition of CRSP for a closing cash payment of approximately $365.0 million, subject to customary adjustments.

In February 2025, we announced plans to retire Morningstar Office, our practice and portfolio management software for registered investment advisors (RIAs). The transition of clients to SS&C's Black Diamond and alternative platforms is underway and is expected to be completed in early 2026.

On October 29, 2025, the board of directors approved a new share repurchase program that authorizes the company to repurchase up to $1.0 billion in shares of the company's outstanding common stock, effective October 31, 2025.

In January 2026, we announced our plans to retire our second-party opinions product by the end of the first quarter as part of a broader effort to sharpen Morningstar Sustainalytics focus on its core data, research and ratings businesses.

tamClaims.claim: Global ratings market totaled approximately $11.8 billion for the trailing 12 months ended September 30, 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that the global ratings market totaled approximately $11.8 billion for the trailing 12 months ended September 30, 2025.
